title: Regression: podman mount (no args): no output
body:
New in podman-0.7.3-1.git0791210.fc28:
```
# podman run --name foo fedora bash -c 'touch /foo'                                                                       
# podman mount foo
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/14df9eb6da1bdad748e99682841c819ba413608bf9843260f47ead2e20e15169/merged
# podman mount
(no output; expected `CID /var/lib/etc/etc`)
```

comments.body:
Side effect of #1104 changing our handling of `podman mount` - we changed the handling of c.state.Mountpoint internally, and now the external consumer I didn't know existed has broken as a result.

We should make `container.Mountpoint()` call directly into containers/storage to determine if the container is mounted
